text: Starting at 2026-05-16T08:14:21, Suricata IDS detected 3 alert(s) from internal host 10.10.44.23 communicating with external IP(s) 185.193.126.44 on port(s) 443. Signature "ET MALWARE Possible C2 Beaconing Activity" was triggered, indicating the host may be infected with malware that is periodically checking in with a command-and-control server (T1071.001 (Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols)). Alert severity is critical (level 1), indicating high-confidence malicious activity. TLS connection observed to "cdn-update-service.com" (issuer: CN=Let's Encrypt Authority X3) with JA3 fingerprint 72a589da586844d7f0818ce684948eea. DNS activity from 10.10.44.23 included 1 query/queries:   • DNS A query for "cdn-update-service.com" The subdomain appears algorithmically generated (DGA), a common C2 technique. The combination of C2 beaconing alerts, suspicious DNS queries, and regular outbound connections from 10.10.44.23 strongly suggests this host is compromised and under active remote control.
